screenshots.scope_note: Current plan card at the top of the Billing page; reachable read-only. scroll_to centers the card. The plan name and limits (Production/Free/etc.) are sample data the compare ignores.

screenshots.depicts: Usage summary for Builds, Updates, and Workflows on the Billing page (Monitor usage)

screenshots.scope_note: EAS Build section on the dedicated Usage page (/settings/usage, confirmed route). scroll_to the EAS Build heading. The chart values and date range are sample data.

screenshots.depicts: Detailed EAS Update usage: monthly active users and global edge bandwidth (EAS Update usage history)
screenshots.scope_note: EAS Update section on the Usage page. scroll_to the EAS Update heading. Chart values are sample data.

screenshots.depicts: Email notification preferences page (Usage notifications)
screenshots.scope_note: The Email notifications page is its own route (/settings/email-notifications, confirmed). The docs link this image to /settings/billing, which is wrong. Full-page capture. Read-only: never toggle a preference.

screenshots.scope_note: Receipts page (/settings/receipts, confirmed). The account has at least one receipt, so the Date/Status/Amount table renders; rows are sample data the compare ignores.

screenshots.depicts: Invoice/receipt page hosted by Stripe (Download and view an invoice)
screenshots.scope_note: External Stripe-hosted page, not an Expo dashboard surface; also needs an existing paid invoice. screenshot-audit only compares Expo dashboard screens.

screenshots.depicts: Receipt row three-dot (kebab) menu opened to reveal Request refund (Request a refund)
reach: On the Receipts page, click the three-dot (kebab) menu at the end of a receipt row to open the menu showing Request refund
screenshots.scope_note: Reachable read-only (the account has a receipt). Opening the kebab menu reveals 'Request refund' to VIEW only, never click it. No testid on the kebab button yet, so this surfaces as NEEDS_REACH; add a clicks selector from the live DOM in the attended pass (do NOT target the Date cell, which is an external Stripe link).

screenshots.depicts: Request a refund form (Request a refund)
reach: Open the receipt kebab menu, then click Request refund to open the form
screenshots.scope_note: The refund form sits two clicks deep (kebab > Request refund) and is the entry to the refund flow. Could be opened read-only, but reaching it reliably plus the mutation-flow context make it a manual eyeball. Out of scope.

screenshots.depicts: Current plan card on the Billing page, with Upcoming bill and Add-ons (View the current plan)
screenshots.scope_note: Same Current plan region as plan-01, from manage.mdx. The doc image shows a Free plan plus the Upcoming bill / Add-ons / right-column links; the plan value and which add-on rows appear are plan-dependent sample data the compare ignores. scroll_to centers the card.

screenshots.depicts: Upgrade plan popup listing Starter, Production, and Enterprise plans (Upgrade to a new plan)
reach: Under Current plan, click Change plan (paid) or See plans (Free) to open the Upgrade plan popup
screenshots.scope_note: Opens the Upgrade plan popup to VIEW the plan list. NEVER click an Upgrade button or select a plan (that enters Checkout). The account is on a paid plan, so the trigger is 'Change plan'; on a Free account it is 'See plans' then 'Select your account', so refine the clicks selector in the attended pass if the popup does not open. Read-only view of the plan list only.

screenshots.depicts: Change plan button under Current plan on the Billing page (Downgrade a plan)
screenshots.scope_note: The Change plan button under Current plan (paid plans show 'Change plan'; Free shows 'See plans'). scroll_to centers it; do NOT click. Visible read-only on the account's paid plan. The doc image's dimmed-background highlight box is an annotation the compare ignores.

The doc image also shows an Upcoming plan block (a scheduled-downgrade state) the live page will not have, which is state, not drift.

settings.scope_note: Current plan section on the Billing page. /settings/billing redirects to the account-scoped path (confirmed in next.config.ts).
Usage estimate appears in the Upcoming bill card on the Billing page (the doc image shows 'Estimate').
The detailed usage breakdown lives on the dedicated Usage page (/settings/usage, confirmed route).
Email notifications page is its own route (/settings/email-notifications, confirmed). Corrected from the docs' /settings/billing link.
Receipts page under Subscription; /settings/receipts redirects to the account-scoped path (confirmed).
Request refund sits behind a per-receipt kebab menu and needs an existing receipt (the account has one). Asserting the label is read-only; never submit a refund.
Under Current plan, the button reads 'Change plan' on a paid plan or 'See plans' on Free. The account is paid, so assert 'Change plan'.
Same Change plan control, documented for the downgrade path; visible read-only on the paid plan.
Upcoming plan only appears after a downgrade has been scheduled (mutation). Out of scope.
Cancel all subscriptions section appears on a paid plan (the account is paid); asserting the label is read-only. Never click through to cancel.
Manage billing information section and the Manage billing link are visible read-only on the Billing page before redirecting to Stripe.
The Add-ons section (Concurrencies) is present on the Billing page (it shows on the Free plan image too, with included concurrencies). Asserting the label is read-only; never click Add.
